    • Making and using bathymetric maps in R with marmap
    • Data import and export strategies in marmap
    • Analysing bathymetric data in R with marmap

Sort bathymetric data matrix by increasing latitude and longitude

Source: R/mar_check_bathy.R
mar_check_bathy.Rd

Reads a bathymetric data matrix and orders its rows and columns by increasing latitude and longitude.

Usage

mar_check_bathy(x)

check.bathy(x)

Arguments

x

a matrix

Value

The output of mar_check_bathy is an ordered matrix.

Details

mar_check_bathy allows to sort rows and columns by increasing latitude and longitude, which is necessary for ploting with the function image (package graphics). mar_check_bathy is used within the marmap functions mar_read_bathy and mar_as_bathy (it is also used in mar_get_noaa_bathy through mar_as_bathy).

See also

mar_read_bathy, mar_as_bathy, mar_get_noaa_bathy

Author

Eric Pante

Examples

matrix(1:100, ncol=5, dimnames=list(20:1, c(3,2,4,1,5))) -> a
mar_check_bathy(a)

On this page
